title: proxy_anthropic_messages is 109-line God function with duplicated OAuth logic
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Architectural Alignment / SRP

Location: src/proxy.rs lines 989-1097 (fn proxy_anthropic_messages).

Violation: This 109-line function performs: (1) body parse, (2) cookie-based OAuth session lookup with find-strip chain, (3) any-valid-session fallback, (4) if OAuth: Claude Code system-prompt injection with three-way match on existing 'system' shape, (5) cache_control TTL stripping, (6) raw URL construction + reqwest call with hardcoded anthropic-beta header string, (7) fallback to API key extraction + forward_to_provider. Cyclomatic complexity high. Two completely separate request-completion paths (OAuth branch returns at line 1077; api-key branch falls through at 1081+).

The OAuth-specific logic (system prompt injection, cache_control stripping, beta header construction) duplicates what claude_credentials.rs::inject_system_prompt and get_oauth_headers already do. There are now THREE functions that know how to construct Anthropic OAuth headers (claude_credentials.rs, proxy.rs::proxy_anthropic_messages, and AnthropicAdapter via x-api-key). Adding a new beta flag requires modifying all three.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. Extract the session resolution into `async fn resolve_anthropic_auth(headers: &HeaderMap, oauth_store: &OAuthStore) -> AnthropicAuth` where AnthropicAuth is an enum { OAuth(OAuthSession), ApiKey(String), None }.
  2. Move the OAuth branch into AnthropicAdapter::oauth_send(client, request, session) — a new method that injects the system prompt via claude_credentials::inject_system_prompt, strips cache_control TTL, and uses claude_credentials::get_oauth_headers.
  3. Delete strip_cache_control_ttl from proxy.rs; move it into claude_credentials.rs alongside inject_system_prompt since they are co-requisites of OAuth requests.
  4. proxy_anthropic_messages becomes ~30 lines: parse, resolve_auth, dispatch to adapter, forward. No inline magic-string headers, no three-way system-shape match.
